headlines: Samsung Collaborations On 2nm And 3D‑IC Put Investor Focus On Valuation Samsung Electronics has expanded its collaborations with Synopsys and Cadence on next-generation chip design and manufacturing flows for its leading 2nm and 3D-IC technologies. The partnerships cover advanced electronic design automation tools, IP, and certified manufacturing processes aimed at AI, high-performance computing, and multi-die silicon solutions. These developments strengthen Samsung's foundry ecosystem at a time when demand for cutting-edge AI and advanced packaging solutions is...

headlines: CrowdStrike Award Underscores AI Security Role And Stretches Rich Valuation CrowdStrike Holdings (NasdaqGS:CRWD) has been named Frost & Sullivan's 2026 Growth and Innovation Leader in Cloud and Application Runtime Security. This is the second consecutive year the company has received this recognition. The award highlights CrowdStrike's role in AI native security infrastructure and the traction of its Falcon platform in securing AI workloads. CrowdStrike's latest recognition lands at a time when the stock has had a strong multi year run and remains in focus with a...

Can CRWD Stock Deliver If The AI Boom Stalls? After a 40% run over the last 12 months, the conversation around CrowdStrike (CRWD) stock is almost entirely about artificial intelligence. You hear about the Mythos inflection point and a new generation of products designed to secure AI deployments. But with that excitement comes a fair question from skeptics: Is this a durable trend, or just a surge of interest and intent from companies still in the early innings of actual AI deployment.

CrowdStrike Named Frost Radar Leader in Cloud Runtime Security for Second Straight Year (CRWD) The cybersecurity company earned the top Growth and Innovation ranking as enterprises increasingly prioritize runtime protection for cloud and AI workloads. Key Investor TakeawaysCrowdStrike (NASDAQ:CRWD) was named the Growth and Innovation Leader in Frost & Sullivan’s 2026 Cloud and Application Runtime Security Radar for the second consecutive year.
